description: In 2006, the Brazilian government launched a massive, nationwide rotavirus vaccination campaign. Despite early skepticism, high levels of government coordination led to rapid public acceptance and a significant drop in infant mortality.

key_events.description: Launch of national vaccination campaign amid initial hesitancy.
National media campaign highlights reduction in hospitalizations.
Ministry of Health releases early data on efficacy and coverage rates.
High vaccination coverage achieved in low-income neighborhoods.

calibration_notes: Excellent model for observing successful public health communication and the conversion of skeptical populations via positive outcomes.
